seo_text.open: Open-air pools in Tashkent are seasonal complexes that operate from May through September. The format suits a full day out with family: sun-lounger terraces, poolside lounge areas, cafes and restaurants, and often a shallower section for kids. Most open-air pools are concentrated in the Bektemir, Sergeli and Yashnabad districts, where spacious plots allow for a pool plus a full leisure area and parking. Entry prices vary by format, from a simple day pass to renting a lounger or table by the water. If you're looking for a place to spend the day with family or friends during the warm season, open-air pools in Tashkent usually offer the widest range of entertainment — from a simple pool with loungers to a full beach-club experience with music and food.
seo_text.indoor: Indoor pools in Tashkent operate year-round regardless of the weather. The water is heated and the facility is equipped with filtration and ventilation systems, making indoor pools the main choice for regular training, coached lessons and therapeutic swimming in winter. Many indoor complexes in the Mirzo-Ulugbek and Chilanzar districts offer monthly memberships, dedicated swimming lanes and group classes. Unlike seasonal outdoor pools, the focus here is less on entertainment and more on the swim itself — water quality and temperature. A solid choice if your goal is consistent training, post-injury recovery, or year-round lessons for kids with a coach.
seo_text.children: Children's pools in Tashkent are designed specifically for kids from age 3, with shallow depth, warm water and play areas. Staff at these facilities are typically trained to work with children, and many offer dedicated programs — coached swim lessons, water games and age-based groups. Parents should pay attention to pool depth and the presence of certified instructors, which are the key safety criteria. Children's pools can be found across the city, from Mirobod to Shayhontohur, and are often combined with larger complexes where parents can relax nearby. A good choice for a child's first introduction to water or regular swim lessons in a safe, supervised environment.
seo_text.sport: Sport pools in Tashkent feature Olympic-length lanes, precise water temperature control and professional coaches. Facilities like the Olympic Sports Complex in Mirzo-Ulugbek or Chekhov Sport Club in Mirobod suit competition training, serious amateur swimming and water polo. Unlike recreational pools, the focus here is on sport infrastructure — lane markings, starting blocks, warm-up areas and locker rooms with showers. Sport pools typically operate year-round and offer memberships for regular visits. If you need a pool for serious training rather than a one-off swim, this category is the place to start.
seo_text.hotel: Hotel pools in Tashkent are typically indoor or seasonal complexes with a higher level of service. Many, including the pools at Radisson Blu, Wyndham, Grand Serena and Lotte City Hotel Tashkent Palace, are open not only to guests but to outside visitors for a separate fee. These pools usually come with extra amenities — a jacuzzi, sauna, spa zone or a bar/restaurant by the water. The format suits those looking for a calmer, more premium experience than a typical city pool — shorter queues and more attentive service, usually at a higher entry price. A good choice for a business meeting by the water or a relaxed evening out.
seo_text.aquapark: Aquaparks in Tashkent are pools with water slides, attractions and wave zones for the whole family. The largest complexes — MONACO (year-round, indoor), Water Park Tashkent, Limpopo and Solnechny Gorod — offer slides of varying height and difficulty, plus separate zones for kids and adults. Most aquaparks in Tashkent operate during the warm season from May to September, though a few year-round indoor options exist for winter visits. The format is great for an active day out with kids — besides the slides, there's usually a cafe, loungers and entertainment. If you're planning a weekend trip with children and want to combine a pool with active fun, an aquapark is generally the best pick among all categories.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Answers to popular questions about swimming pools in Tashkent
faq.meta_title: FAQ — Tashkent Swimming Pools: prices, memberships, schedule | Basen.uz
faq.meta_desc: Answers to 9 questions about Tashkent swimming pools: average prices, memberships, where to swim in winter, free pools — for 2026.
faq.q1: How do I find the nearest pool?
faq.a1: Open the Map page — all pools are shown on an interactive map of Tashkent. You can filter by category and see their location relative to any point.
faq.q2: What is the average price for a single visit?
faq.a2: Prices range from 30,000 to 150,000 UZS per visit. Open-air and children's pools are usually cheaper; sports complexes and hotel pools tend to be pricier.
faq.q3: Can I buy a membership online?
faq.a3: Currently, memberships are purchased at the pool's front desk. On Basen.uz you can check prices and schedules in advance so you don't waste time calling.
faq.a4: Many pools offer professional coaching services. Use the 'Coach' filter in the catalog to find pools that have instructors.
faq.a5: Outdoor pools in Tashkent operate seasonally — typically from May through September. Indoor pools and aquaparks are open year-round.
faq.q6: How do I add my pool to the site?
faq.a6: Message us on Telegram — we'll verify the information and add your pool to the catalog for free.
faq.q7: Where can I swim in Tashkent in winter?
faq.a7: In winter, seasonal open-air pools close, but indoor pools and heated aquaparks keep operating — for example, MONACO in the Mirzo-Ulugbek district or the Water Sports Palace in Yunusabad. The catalog has around 18 year-round venues in total — filter the catalog by the 'Year-round' season to see all options.
faq.q8: Are there any free pools in Tashkent?
faq.a8: Fully free pools are rare — for example, entry to Malibu Sun Club is free, you only pay for a lounger or table rental. In most cases you'll need to pay for pool entry, but prices for children's and open-air pools are usually lower than for sports complexes and hotel pools.
faq.q9: How much does a monthly pool membership cost in Tashkent?
faq.a9: A monthly membership usually costs between 350,000 and 1,200,000 UZS, depending on the pool, service level, and whether coaching is included. Check the exact price on each pool's page under 'Prices'.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.subtitle: How we handle your data
privacy.meta_desc: Privacy Policy of Basen.uz — Tashkent pool catalog.
privacy.s1_text: This Privacy Policy applies to Basen.uz — an online catalog of swimming pools in Tashkent. We respect your privacy and do not collect personal data unnecessarily.
privacy.s2_text: Basen.uz is an informational resource. We do not require registration and do not collect personal data (name, email, phone) through site forms.
privacy.s2_cookie_text: The site may use technical cookies necessary for proper operation (e.g., saving the selected language). We may also use web analytics services (Yandex.Metrica, Google Analytics) to collect anonymized traffic statistics.
privacy.s3_text: The site uses the following third-party services, which may collect data according to their own privacy policies:
privacy.s3_maps: Mapping service for displaying an interactive map of pools
privacy.s3_analytics: Yandex.Metrica / Google Analytics — for traffic analysis (anonymized data)
privacy.s4_text: Anonymized analytics data is used solely to improve the site: analyzing popular pages, fixing errors, and improving usability. We do not sell or share any data with third parties for commercial purposes.
privacy.s5_text: The site contains links to pool pages, social networks, and messengers (Telegram). We are not responsible for the privacy policies of these resources.
privacy.s6_text: Since we do not collect personal data, deletion or modification requests are not required. If you have questions — contact us.
privacy.s7_text: We reserve the right to update this policy. The current version is always available on this page. Continued use of the site constitutes agreement with the current version.
privacy.s8_text: For privacy-related questions, please reach out:

terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.subtitle: Rules for using the Basen.uz website
terms.meta_desc: Terms of Use for Basen.uz — Tashkent pool catalog.
terms.s1_text: By using Basen.uz, you agree to these Terms of Use. If you do not agree with any provision, please stop using the site.
terms.s2_text: Basen.uz is a free informational catalog of swimming pools in Tashkent. The site provides users with information about pools: addresses, prices, schedules, services, and contact details. We are not an intermediary and do not sell tickets or memberships.
terms.s3_text: We make efforts to keep data up to date, but do not guarantee absolute accuracy of prices, schedules, and descriptions. Before visiting, we recommend verifying information directly with the pool by phone or messenger.
terms.s4_text: All site materials (texts, design, logo, structure) are the property of Basen.uz. Copying and using materials without written permission is prohibited. Exception — personal non-commercial use.
terms.s5_text: By using the site, you agree not to disrupt the service, not to use automated tools for data scraping, not to spread false information, and not to violate the current legislation of the Republic of Uzbekistan.
terms.s6_text: The site contains links to external resources (pool websites, maps, messengers). Basen.uz is not responsible for the content, availability, or policies of third-party sites.
terms.s7_text: Basen.uz is provided "as is". We are not liable for direct or indirect damages arising from the use or inability to use the site, or from discrepancies between information and actual conditions.
terms.s8_text: We reserve the right to change these Terms at any time. The current version is always available at basen.uz/terms. Continued use of the site after changes constitutes agreement with the new terms.
terms.s9_text: These Terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of Uzbekistan. All disputes are resolved pre-trial through negotiation.
terms.s10_text: For questions related to the use of the site:
